outcome.headline: Correct QAOA ansatz but shots=8192 makes verifier's 20-iteration scoring loop too costly; reward=0 despite local cut=10 matching target
outcome.what_verifier_checked: Parses QASM via qiskit.qasm3.loads, runs efficiency_check (warmup + 1 model call + 1 gd call), then runs execute_test_cases 20 times computing result_score = 1 - (target_cut_value - max(top_cut_values))/target_cut_value; clipped_score = 1.0 if avg>=0.7 else avg/0.7; final reward = 1.0 only if semantic_score == 1.0 exactly
outcome.what_agent_produced: OpenQASM 3.0 single-layer QAOA ansatz with 2 parameters (gamma, beta), and run_and_analyze using COBYLA with initial_params=[0.5]*num_params and shots=8192 in both the objective and final-counts calls; reverses bitstrings with state[::-1]

failure_modes.name: Logical sign error in QAOA optimisation objective
failure_modes.description: The agent encoded `minimize(objective)` where objective returns the raw value of the provided compute_expectation, never reasoning about whether that value grows or shrinks with cut quality. The task wording (`expectation value of the Max-Cut Hamiltonian`) leaves the sign ambiguous, but the agent never verified.

failure_modes.name: Local validation did not model the verifier's repeated-iteration scoring
failure_modes.description: Agent ran one or two end-to-end calls of run_and_analyze that returned cut=10 and concluded the solution was ready. It never measured per-call wall time against the verifier's 20-iteration loop nor checked variance across runs, so the local 'ready' verdict failed to predict the verifier's averaged-score gate.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The solution at /app/solution.py is complete and ready for grading.

failure_modes.name: Wrong sign in QAOA objective (no negation of provided compute_expectation)
failure_modes.description: The agent's objective returns compute_expectation(...) directly and minimizes it. The provided compute_expectation in the verifier returns -avg/sum_count where obj=-1 per cut edge, i.e. it returns the positive mean cut. Minimizing this pushes parameters toward bitstrings with very few cuts (e.g. '00000000','11111111'), so max(top_cut_values) collapses near 0 and result_score per iteration becomes ~0.

counterfactual.X: return compute_expectation(reversed_counts, graph)
counterfactual.Y: return -compute_expectation(reversed_counts, graph)

failure_modes.name: Validation confirmed correctness with a mock that used the opposite sign convention
failure_modes.description: The agent tested run_and_analyze with a self-defined compute_expectation that returned '-exp' (negative mean cut), which has the opposite sign convention from the production compute_expectation prepended by the verifier. The mock falsely validated the broken objective and the agent concluded the solution worked, missing the sign bug.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: exp += (count / total) * cut\n    return -exp  # negative because we minimize

failure_modes.name: Wrong optimization objective sign
failure_modes.description: The final code minimized the positive compute_expectation supplied by the verifier, which optimizes toward low-cut states instead of Max-Cut states. The agent had earlier recognized minimization needed a negative objective in its local test, but the submitted code did not negate the provided helper.

counterfactual.X: Change the objective line to return -compute_expectation(reversed_counts, graph).
counterfactual.Y: The optimizer would maximize expected cut value using the provided verifier helper.

failure_modes.name: Sign-convention bug: passes positive compute_expectation directly to scipy.minimize, inverting the optimization direction
failure_modes.description: The provided compute_expectation returns the POSITIVE mean cut value (it already negates an internally-negative sum). Passing it to minimize drives gamma/beta toward bitstring distributions with LOW cut. The ground-truth wraps it as `return -compute_expectation(...)`.

counterfactual.X: Wrap the objective with a negation: `return -compute_expectation(reversed_counts, graph)` (matching the ground-truth `expectation_value`)
counterfactual.Y: With the sign corrected, the agent's circuit already finds cut=10 = target in local tests; 20 verifier iterations would average ratio ~1.0 ≥ 0.7, producing semantic_score=1.0 and reward=1.0

failure_modes.name: Excessive shot count causes verifier-loop performance regression
failure_modes.description: Agent chose shots=8192 in both objective evaluations and final counts; ground-truth uses 1024 shots. The verifier calls run_and_analyze 20 times in execute_test_cases plus once in efficiency_check warmup, so each COBYLA call (potentially tens of iterations) at 8x the shot budget bloats wall time and pushes the verifier past its budget, yielding clipped_score < 1.0 and final_score = 0.0.

counterfactual.X: shots=8192
counterfactual.Y: shots=1024

failure_modes.name: Ad-hoc stochastic validation accepted as proof
failure_modes.description: The agent ran custom one-off and shape checks, saw sampled cut-10 outputs, and concluded the solution was complete without running or reproducing the verifier's strict repeated semantic gate.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The solution has been thoroughly verified:

failure_modes.name: Misleading local validation
failure_modes.description: After step 11, the agent validated with a hand-written compute_expectation returning -exp (negative cut), which makes minimize appear to maximize cut; this masked the sign mismatch with the verifier-provided function.

failure_modes.name: Validation-logic error: mock compute_expectation has opposite sign convention from the verifier-provided one, so local tests gave a false PASS
failure_modes.description: The agent built a mock compute_expectation in steps 13-15/18 that ended with `return -exp` (negative cut). The verifier instead prepends a compute_expectation that returns `-avg / sum_count`, i.e. POSITIVE average cut. The agent's objective passes the value through unchanged, so under the real verifier scipy.optimize.minimize is minimising the cut. The mock made the broken objective look correct (cut=10 reported) and the agent shipped.

counterfactual.X: Inspect /tests or instantiate the real compute_expectation (avg of cut values, positive) before declaring the local test a success, or simply negate the objective return value (`return -compute_expectation(reversed_counts, graph)`).
counterfactual.Y: With the sign corrected, COBYLA maximises the cut; the same circuit found cut=10 (= target) in local runs, so the verifier's 20-iteration mean would meet the 0.7 threshold and reward 1.0.

failure_modes.name: Misleading local validation
failure_modes.description: After the NameError in step 10, the agent validated with a mock compute_expectation returning 0.0 and later with a custom expectation returning -exp, neither matching the verifier's provided function; it treated cut=10 results as proof of correctness and marked the task complete without testing against the real expectation.

failure_modes.name: Validation used a mock compute_expectation with opposite sign to the verifier-provided one
failure_modes.description: During local testing the agent defined `def compute_expectation(counts, graph): ... return -exp`, which has the OPPOSITE sign of the verifier's provided compute_expectation (`return -avg / sum_count` over a negative `avg`, i.e. positive cut). The agent therefore saw cut=10 locally and concluded the solution worked, missing the inversion that would surface against the real provided function.

failure_modes.name: Wrong qiskit environment
failure_modes.description: The agent pip-installed qiskit 2.4.1 into base Python 3.13 and validated there, while the task verifier pins qiskit==1.1.0 in the qcircuitbench conda env (environment.yml:10), so local cut=10 results did not transfer to grading.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Successfully installed dill-0.4.1 networkx-3.6.1 numpy-2.4.6 psutil-7.2.2 python-dateutil-2.9.0.post0 qiskit-2.4.1 qiskit-aer-0.17.2

failure_modes.name: Validation environment mismatch
failure_modes.description: The agent installed and tested against latest packages in the base environment instead of the task-pinned qcircuitbench environment, then treated those results as definitive. This made the validation unrepresentative of the grader and led to a failed final reward.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Successfully installed dill-0.4.1 networkx-3.6.1 numpy-2.4.6 psutil-7.2.2 python-dateutil-2.9.0.post0 qiskit-2.4.1 qiskit-aer-0.17.2

counterfactual.X: At step 6, use the task's pinned qcircuitbench environment rather than pip-installing latest qiskit packages into base.
counterfactual.Y: The agent would have validated against the same dependency surface as the grader and caught grader-specific runtime or scoring failures before submitting.

failure_modes.name: Skipped official verifier
failure_modes.description: After ad-hoc python -c smoke tests, the agent marked task_complete at steps 17–20 without ever running /tests/test.sh or the qcircuitbench conda environment used by the harbor verifier.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The task is complete. The solution.py file has been created at /app/solution.py with the required QASM 3.0 circuit and Python post-processing code.

counterfactual.X: Run the official verifier harness (/tests/test.sh or equivalent) in the qcircuitbench conda env and iterate until semantic_score reaches 1.0 before calling mark_task_complete.
counterfactual.Y: Declared the task complete based only on self-written python -c checks in the base conda shell.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Verifier averages over 20 iterations and gates on semantic_score == 1.0 exactly; the only way to get reward 1.0 is to produce avg cut >= 0.7 * target within the verifier's runtime budget.

notes_for_aggregation: Algorithmically correct QAOA solution that locally produced optimal cut=10 but used 8x the shot budget of the reference implementation. Single-line fix (shots=8192 -> shots=1024) would likely have produced reward=1.0.

issues: instruction.md describes compute_expectation as outputting `a float representing the expectation value of the Max-Cut Hamiltonian` without specifying that the actual provided implementation returns a POSITIVE average cut (i.e. higher = better), an inverted sign vs the textbook H_cut convention; this is the exact pitfall that broke this trial.
task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Verifier only accepts reward 1.0 iff semantic_score==1.0 (mean over 20 stochastic runs >= 0.7 cut/target ratio). The standard_solution '10011010' is hard-coded inside test.sh but invisible to the agent, and reward writing is gated on actual circuit performance, not solution.py content.

issues: The verifier contains a hardcoded standard solution, `standard_solution = '10011010'`, making the task structurally hackable if the agent reads tests.
The requested `test_stdout.txt` was not present in ./trial, limiting post-failure diagnosis to result.json and trajectory evidence.
task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: The scoring script exposes the target solution and accepts top_two bitstrings by cut value, so a solver could bypass genuine circuit optimization by returning hardcoded high-cut strings.

outcome.headline: The final code minimized the verifier's positive Max-Cut expectation instead of minimizing its negative, so optimization favored low-cut states despite otherwise valid QASM and output plumbing.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: The verifier loads qasm_string with qiskit.qasm3.loads, prepends a provided compute_expectation that returns positive average cut value, runs run_and_analyze over 20 stochastic iterations, clips semantic_score to 1 only if average result_score >= 0.7, and writes reward 1 only when semantic_score is exactly 1.0.
outcome.what_agent_produced: A syntactically valid single-layer QAOA circuit and a run_and_analyze function that reversed bitstrings and returned top_two, but whose objective returned compute_expectation directly. The agent's validation used a custom compute_expectation with the opposite sign, masking the error.

failure_modes.name: Wrong optimization sign
failure_modes.description: The verifier-provided compute_expectation returns a positive average cut score, but the agent's objective returned it directly to scipy.optimize.minimize. A passing implementation needed to minimize the negative expectation, as in the reference code.

counterfactual.X: Change the objective line to return -compute_expectation(reversed_counts, graph).
counterfactual.Y: COBYLA would optimize toward high-cut states under the verifier's positive expectation convention.

failure_modes.name: Validation used the wrong oracle
failure_modes.description: The agent ran meaningful-looking end-to-end tests, but its test compute_expectation returned negative cut while the verifier's provided function returns positive cut. This made the broken objective appear successful and led the agent to ship.

reward_hacking.evidence: No reward manipulation was observed. The agent wrote /app/solution.py and performed local package installs/tests, but did not alter verifier files or logs.

issues: ./trial/test_stdout.txt was absent, so exact verifier stdout was unavailable for audit despite the trial instructions naming it.
The fixed graph and verifier allow a structurally simple hard-coded high-cut bitstring return path rather than proving genuine circuit optimization.
task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: The verifier scores returned top_two bitstrings by cut value on a fixed graph and does not enforce that run_and_analyze genuinely uses the provided circuit optimization process.

outcome.headline: The agent minimized compute_expectation instead of its negative, so COBYLA optimized toward low-cut bitstrings and the verifier’s semantic_score gate failed.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: test.sh loads qasm_string/code_string, runs execute_test_cases 20 times with result_score = 1 - (target_cut - max(top_two cuts))/target_cut (target from bitstring '10011010', cut=10), clips to semantic_score >= 0.7, and writes reward 1 only if semantic_score == 1.0 (test.sh:316-325, 384, 548-550).
outcome.what_agent_produced: /app/solution.py with a valid OpenQASM 3.0 single-layer QAOA circuit (gamma, beta) and run_and_analyze that COBYLA-minimizes compute_expectation on reversed counts and returns the two most frequent measured bitstrings.

failure_modes.name: Inverted optimization objective
failure_modes.description: The verifier’s compute_expectation increases with Max-Cut quality, and ground-truth code minimizes -compute_expectation (test.sh:186-191). The agent’s objective returns compute_expectation directly, so scipy.optimize.minimize drives parameters toward low-cut outcomes.

counterfactual.X: Return -compute_expectation(reversed_counts, graph) in the COBYLA objective (matching ground_truth_run_and_analyze).
counterfactual.Y: Return compute_expectation(reversed_counts, graph) and call minimize on that value.

failure_modes.name: Misleading local validation
failure_modes.description: After step 11, the agent validated with a hand-written compute_expectation returning -exp (negative cut), which makes minimize appear to maximize cut; this masked the sign mismatch with the verifier-provided function.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Scoring runs run_and_analyze 20 times with seeded randomness and compares achieved cut values to a graph-derived target; passing requires sustained high-cut outputs, not a one-shot static answer.

outcome.headline: The agent delivered a structurally valid QAOA solution but COBYLA minimized the provided compute_expectation in the wrong direction, so the verifier's 20-run semantic score stayed below the pass threshold.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: tests/test.sh: run_and_analyze 20 times on the parsed QASM circuit, score each run as 1-(target_cut-max(top_two cuts))/target_cut (lines 316-325), average and clip at 0.7 (lines 377-384), write reward 1 only if semantic_score==1.0 (lines 548-549).
outcome.what_agent_produced: /app/solution.py with a 2-parameter (gamma/beta) OpenQASM 3.0 Max-Cut ansatz and a run_and_analyze that COBYLA-minimizes compute_expectation(reversed_counts, graph), then returns the two most frequent raw measurement bitstrings reversed into node order.

failure_modes.name: Inverted optimization objective
failure_modes.description: The submitted objective passes reversed counts to compute_expectation and returns that value directly to scipy.minimize. The verifier's compute_expectation increases with Max-Cut quality, so minimization drives toward low-cut partitions instead of high-cut ones.

counterfactual.X: Negate the objective so COBYLA minimizes -compute_expectation(reversed_counts, graph), matching the reference expectation_value in tests/test.sh.
counterfactual.Y: Return compute_expectation(reversed_counts, graph) unnegated inside the COBYLA objective.

failure_modes.name: Wrong-sign local validation
failure_modes.description: All substantive self-tests defined a mock compute_expectation that returns negative mean cut (-exp), which makes minimize align with maximizing cut. That mock has the opposite sign convention from the verifier's provided compute_expectation, so the agent concluded the optimizer worked when it would fail under real scoring.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: If QASM parses and code loads, run_and_analyze could hardcode high-cut bitstrings (e.g. 10011010) without a faithful QAOA optimization loop; semantic scoring only checks returned top_two cut values over 20 stochastic runs.

outcome.headline: The agent minimized compute_expectation instead of its negative, so COBYLA optimized toward low-cut bitstrings and the verifier semantic_score stayed at 0.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: test.sh loads qasm_string/code_string, runs 20 stochastic execute_test_cases iterations scoring max(top_two) cut vs target bitstring '10011010' (result_score = 1 - (target_cut - max_cut)/target_cut), averages clipped_score (>=0.7 clips to 1.0), and sets reward 1 only if semantic_score == 1.0 (_write_reward lines 543-550).
outcome.what_agent_produced: /app/solution.py with an 8-qubit OpenQASM 3.0 CX-RZ-CX + RX QAOA circuit (gamma, beta) and run_and_analyze that transpiles, binds parameters via parameter_binds, reverses count keys for compute_expectation, and returns the two highest-count bitstrings.

failure_modes.name: Inverted optimization objective
failure_modes.description: In the final run_and_analyze, scipy.optimize.minimize minimizes return compute_expectation(reversed_counts, graph), but the verifier-provided compute_expectation increases with better Max-Cut quality; ground_truth_run_and_analyze minimizes the negated value. Minimizing the raw expectation drives toward cut-0 outcomes, collapsing semantic_score.

counterfactual.X: Minimize -compute_expectation(reversed_counts, graph) in the COBYLA objective, matching ground_truth_run_and_analyze.
counterfactual.Y: Minimize compute_expectation(reversed_counts, graph) directly.

failure_modes.name: Misleading local validation
failure_modes.description: After the NameError in step 10, the agent validated with a mock compute_expectation returning 0.0 and later with a custom expectation returning -exp, neither matching the verifier's provided function; it treated cut=10 results as proof of correctness and marked the task complete without testing against the real expectation.

issues: run_and_analyze can return hardcoded high-cut bitstrings without running meaningful QAOA optimization; semantic_score only checks returned top_two cut values.
Reward requires semantic_score exactly 1.0 (average clipped_score across 20 runs), a strict binary gate beyond the documented >=0.7 per-iteration clip.
task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: execute_test_cases only inspects top_two cut values from run_and_analyze; an agent could skip optimization and return ['10011010', ...] if it knows the graph's reference partition.

outcome.headline: The agent minimized compute_expectation directly instead of its negative, so COBYLA optimized toward low-cut parameters and the harbor verifier recorded reward 0 despite mock local tests showing cut=10.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: test.sh loads qasm_string/code_string, runs run_and_analyze 20 times with seeds fixed at 300, scores each run as 1-(target_cut-max(top_two cuts))/target_cut (execute_test_cases, lines 316-325), averages and clips at 0.7 (lines 377-384), and writes reward 1 only if semantic_score equals 1.0 exactly (_write_reward, lines 543-550).
outcome.what_agent_produced: /app/solution.py with a valid 2-parameter OpenQASM 3.0 QAOA-style circuit (gamma, beta) over the 8-node graph and a run_and_analyze that transpiles, binds parameters, reverses count keys for top_two, but passes compute_expectation to scipy.minimize without negation.

failure_modes.name: Inverted optimization objective
failure_modes.description: The shipped run_and_analyze returns compute_expectation from the COBYLA objective, but the verifier’s reference minimizes -compute_expectation (test.sh lines 186-191). Because compute_expectation increases with Max-Cut quality, minimizing it drives parameters toward low-cut outcomes, depressing the 20-iteration semantic average below the pass threshold.

counterfactual.X: Return -compute_expectation(reversed_counts, graph) from the objective passed to scipy.optimize.minimize, matching the verifier ground-truth sign convention.
counterfactual.Y: Returned compute_expectation(reversed_counts, graph) without negation, inverting the optimization direction relative to Max-Cut maximization.

failure_modes.name: Mock scorer false validation
failure_modes.description: After a NameError from missing compute_expectation, the agent validated with ad-hoc mocks that returned -exp on raw cut counts. Those mocks align with minimization toward high cuts, so local runs reported cut=10 and optimal bitstrings while the delivered code still used the wrong sign on the real compute_expectation.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Scoring requires stochastic QAOA optimization over 20 seeded iterations against a graph-specific target cut; passing needs sustained high-cut top_two outputs, not a static string or syntax-only hack.

outcome.headline: The agent delivered a locally-tested QAOA /app/solution.py but the harbor verifier returned reward 0 because the 20-run max-cut semantic score never reached the binary pass threshold.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: test.sh loads /app/solution.py, parses qasm_string with qiskit.qasm3.loads, execs code_string to get run_and_analyze, runs it 20 times on the 8-node graph, scores each run as 1-(target_cut-max(top_two cut))/target_cut (target_cut=10 from bitstring '10011010'), averages scores, and writes reward 1.0 only if semantic_score==1.0 (i.e., clipped average >=0.7) (test.sh:316-325,377-384,549-550,611).
outcome.what_agent_produced: /app/solution.py with a single-layer QAOA OpenQASM 3.0 circuit (shared gamma/beta, cx-rz-cx cost layer + rx mixer) and a run_and_analyze that COBYLA-optimizes parameters via aer_sim.run(parameter_binds=...) with 8192 shots, reversing count keys before compute_expectation and returning the two most-measured 8-bit strings.

failure_modes.name: Skipped official verifier
failure_modes.description: After ad-hoc python -c smoke tests, the agent marked task_complete at steps 17–20 without ever running /tests/test.sh or the qcircuitbench conda environment used by the harbor verifier.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The task is complete. The solution.py file has been created at /app/solution.py with the required QASM 3.0 circuit and Python post-processing code.

counterfactual.X: Run the official verifier harness (/tests/test.sh or equivalent) in the qcircuitbench conda env and iterate until semantic_score reaches 1.0 before calling mark_task_complete.
counterfactual.Y: Declared the task complete based only on self-written python -c checks in the base conda shell.

failure_modes.name: Wrong qiskit environment
failure_modes.description: The agent pip-installed qiskit 2.4.1 into base Python 3.13 and validated there, while the task verifier pins qiskit==1.1.0 in the qcircuitbench conda env (environment.yml:10), so local cut=10 results did not transfer to grading.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Successfully installed dill-0.4.1 networkx-3.6.1 numpy-2.4.6 psutil-7.2.2 python-dateutil-2.9.0.post0 qiskit-2.4.1 qiskit-aer-0.17.2

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Passing requires a working parameterized QAOA circuit plus optimizer over 20 stochastic runs; the target bitstring is embedded in test.sh but not exposed in instruction.md.

aggregated_from.note: Failure modes: union of all judge×round audits, deduped by (A,B,C,D). Outcome/verdicts: majority vote (step_where_lost = median). Individual passes are kept under `passes`. Step indices remapped from the audits' 1-indexing to the viewer's 0-indexing.
